products.title: Black Spot Rescue
products.lede: Rescue your plants from black spot and promote their health with our handmade Black Spot Rescue. Order now and experience the gentle yet effective power of thyme for a thriving, organic garden.
products.body: Effective Black Spot Treatment: Our Black Spot Rescue is specifically formulated to rescue plants from black spots. Hand-distilled with the detoxifying thyme herb, it offers a gentle yet effective solution for plant health. Handmade Quality: Crafted with care in our Norfolk workshop, this plant tonic harnesses the active principles of thyme, providing your plants with the best natural care. Safe for All Plants: This tonic is safe to use on all types of plants, including edibles. It's pet-safe, bee-safe, and organic, ensuring a healthy garden ecosystem. Natural Ingredients: Made with aqua (water), Thymus Vulgaris (thyme) oil distillate, Gluconolactone and Sodium Benzoate (GSB), and a natural preservative, this tonic promotes plant health without harmful chemicals. Easy to Use: Simply remove any infected leaves, shake the bottle well, and spritz directly onto the affected areas and soil beneath. For optimal results, use twice weekly for a minimum of six weeks.

products.lede: Create a beautiful, bee-friendly wildflower patch with these 100 seed balls. Perfect for attracting solitary bees, honey bees, and bumblebees. A sustainable way to brighten your garden while supporting pollinators.
products.body: Transform your garden into a haven for bees with our pack of 100 wildflower seed balls. Each bag covers 5 square meters and contains a thoughtfully selected mix of bee-friendly plants, including Birdsfoot Trefoil, Foxglove, Red Clover, Viper’s Bugloss, Wild Marjoram, Chamomile, Cornflower, Corn Marigold, and Night-flowering Catchfly. These plants are all specially chosen for their ability to attract solitary bees, honey bees, and bumblebees, with the mix certified by the Bumblebee Conservation Trust. These seed balls are made from a mixture of native UK wildflower seeds, clay, peat-free compost, and a touch of chilli powder to deter slugs and snails. The clay protects the seeds from wildlife, and once watered, the seeds will germinate and grow into beautiful wildflowers. The eco-friendly, plastic-free packaging ensures a sustainable solution for your garden, while clear instructions on the back of the pack guide you through planting. Best scattered in Spring or Autumn, these seed balls are a perfect way to create larger wildflower patches and encourage biodiversity. Eco-friendly information Self-reported by the brand Product materials Upcycled Made from over 50% reused by-products and waste. Packaging Recyclable Consumers can recycle the packaging at most recycling facilities. Made in the United Kingdom.

Included in the mix are Purple Loosestrife, Forget-me-not, Musk Mallow, Red Campion, and Yarrow, along with a touch of pollinator-friendly annuals like Chamomile, Cornflower, Corn Marigold, and Night-flowering Catchfly. These seed balls are made from a mixture of UK native wildflower seeds, clay, peat-free compost, and chilli powder. The clay acts as a protective barrier against wildlife, and once watered, the seeds inside will germinate, nourished by the minerals and nutrients within the ball. The chilli powder helps deter slugs and snails. Packaged in compostable, plastic-free paper, this product is not only great for the environment but also supports local biodiversity. Full instructions are included on the back of the pack, and the best time to scatter these seed balls is in Spring or Autumn. products.lede: When we breathe in the scent of a flower it’s as though we are drinking in all the delights of Nature. It’s well known that some scents evoke poignant memories and we all have our favourites – and our less favourites! The aromas in a flower – or any other part of the plant – come primarily from their essential oils.
products.body: Some oils help the plants repel predators or protect themselves from disease, others are designed to attract beneficial insects – and humans it seems – offering valuable rewards in return for pollination – or for growing in your garden. We have chosen three herbs for The Scented Garden with truly delicious smells. Roman chamomile is fresh and fruity, bringing balance and harmony (it’s different from the German chamomile we usually drink as a herbal tea); lavender is full of summer’s sweet and relaxing balm; and clary sage’s sublime delicacy lets our spirit soar. For the optimum perfumer’s pleasure, enjoy them every day when they are in flower.
